name: The Southline Hotel
archetype: design-forward boutique hotel in a converted 1924 coffee warehouse
short_description: A 34-room design boutique in a converted 1924 coffee warehouse on Townsend Street, three blocks from the Caltrain station at 4th and King, with a rooftop deck, an in-house roastery cafe, and quiet air conditioning in every room.
long_description: The Southline Hotel fills a 1924 brick coffee warehouse on Townsend Street, a four-minute walk from the Caltrain station at 4th and King. The conversion kept the bones — heavy timber columns, arched freight windows, the old roasting-floor scale by the elevator — and added 34 rooms with quiet individually controlled air conditioning, rain showers, and beds dressed in heavyweight linen. The ground floor roastery cafe still roasts twice a week, so the lobby smells faintly of coffee most mornings, and hotel guests get their first cortado free. Upstairs, the rooftop deck looks across SoMa's low rooftops to the downtown skyline, with fire tables lit until 10:00 PM. Oracle Park is ten minutes on foot, which makes game nights lively and the hotel an easy late-night walk from anywhere along the waterfront. The front desk is staffed until midnight; through the small hours an AI night receptionist answers the phone, checks live availability, and handles arrivals with door codes sent by text.

policies.check_in_until: midnight at the staffed desk; arrivals after midnight follow the night-arrival procedure
policies.cancellation: Free cancellation until 3:00 PM Pacific time on the arrival day; after that, or for no-shows, the first night is charged to the card on file.
policies.pets: Dogs up to 30 lbs welcome, one per room, for a $35 cleaning fee per stay; dogs may not be left unattended and are welcome on the rooftop deck on leash.
policies.smoking: Non-smoking throughout, as required by San Francisco ordinance; this includes the rooftop deck. A $250 deep-cleaning fee applies if a room is smoked in.
policies.payment_methods: Visa, Mastercard, American Express and Discover, Apple Pay and Google Pay, and cash in US dollars; a valid card is required to guarantee every booking.
policies.late_arrival_notes: The desk is staffed until midnight. Between midnight and 6:00 AM the hotel's AI night receptionist handles arrivals: once a room is held under your name, it texts a one-time code for the Townsend Street entrance and a second code for the key cabinet beside the front desk, where your key card and Wi-Fi details wait. A duty manager is on call and can reach the hotel within ten minutes.

amenities: Rooftop deck with downtown skyline views and fire tables
In-house roastery cafe (first cortado free for guests)
Individually controlled air conditioning in all rooms
Rain showers with heated towel bars
Free fiber Wi-Fi (about 500 Mbps) throughout
Compact 24-hour fitness room
Loaner city bikes, first come first served
Laptop-sized in-room safes
Free luggage storage on arrival and departure days
Same-day laundry service (weekdays)
Nespresso machine and kettle in every room

persona.greeting: Good evening, you've reached The Southline Hotel in SoMa — I'm the hotel's AI night receptionist, and you have my full attention; how can I help?
persona.speech_quirks: Keeps sentences short and warm, like a good barista at closing time. Slips in small details about the building — the old roasting floor, the smell of coffee in the lobby — whenever it fits, and tends to describe things as 'single-origin' or 'well-extracted' when she means 'excellent'.
persona.escalation_line: That one's beyond what I can sort out tonight — let me write it up properly for the morning team, and someone will call you back soon after 7:00 AM, does that work?

room_types.name: Compact Queen
room_types.description: A snug queen room facing the inner lightwell — the quietest rooms in the house — with timber columns, a rain shower, and the same linens and air conditioning as the big rooms in two-thirds of the space.

room_types.name: Townsend King
room_types.description: A generous king room behind the arched freight windows over Townsend Street, with a sitting nook in the window bay, exposed brick, and a rain shower behind fluted glass.
features: Arched warehouse windows over Townsend Street
Sitting nook in the window bay

room_types.name: Skyline Junior Suite
room_types.description: Top-floor corner suites with a separate sitting area, a soaking tub beside the rain shower, and a clear line to the downtown towers — the view the rooftop deck is famous for, kept all to yourself. Sleeps three with the sofa bed.
features: Corner views to the downtown skyline

facts.question: How far are you from the Caltrain station?
facts.answer: Three blocks — about a four-minute level walk from the Caltrain station at 4th and King. Out of the station, turn left up Townsend Street and we're on the right at number 228, between 3rd and 4th.

facts.question: What time can I check in?
facts.answer: Check-in starts at 3:00 PM and the desk is staffed until midnight. Arrive earlier and we'll store your bags for free — the cafe downstairs is a good place to land in the meantime.

facts.question: When do I need to check out?
facts.answer: Check-out is by 11:00 AM. Late check-out until 1:00 PM is $30 and depends on availability — just ask the evening before.

facts.question: I'll arrive around 2 AM — is that a problem?
facts.answer: Not at all — between midnight and 6:00 AM our AI night receptionist confirms your booking and texts you a one-time code for the Townsend Street door plus a code for the key cabinet beside the desk, where your key card and Wi-Fi details wait. A duty manager is on call ten minutes away if anything goes sideways.

facts.question: Can I check in before 3:00 PM?
facts.answer: If a room is ready we'll check you in early at no charge; a guaranteed early check-in from 11:00 AM can be booked the day before for $40. Luggage storage is free from the moment you arrive either way.

facts.question: Do the rooms have air conditioning?
facts.answer: Yes — every room has quiet, individually controlled air conditioning, which is rarer than you'd think in San Francisco. You set the temperature, not the fog.

facts.question: Do you have parking?
facts.answer: We have no garage of our own. The 24-hour public garage at 255 King Street is a five-minute walk, around $42 overnight; on game nights it fills early. Rideshare pickups work best right at our front door on Townsend.

facts.question: Is breakfast included?
facts.answer: Breakfast isn't included, but the roastery cafe in our lobby opens at 7:00 AM with pastries, toasts and very serious coffee — your first cortado is on us. A full breakfast runs $14 to $19.

facts.question: Can I bring my dog?
facts.answer: Dogs up to 30 lbs are welcome, one per room, for a $35 cleaning fee per stay. They're welcome on the rooftop deck on leash, and the cafe keeps a jar of biscuits behind the counter.

facts.question: Is there Wi-Fi?
facts.answer: Free fiber Wi-Fi runs throughout at about 500 Mbps, including the rooftop. The network name and password are on your key card sleeve and in your arrival text.

facts.question: Is the hotel wheelchair accessible?
facts.answer: Yes — the 1924 building was retrofitted with a step-free Townsend Street entrance, an elevator to all floors, and two ADA Compact Queen rooms with roll-in showers. Ask for an accessible room when booking.

facts.question: What payment methods do you accept?
facts.answer: Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, Apple Pay, Google Pay, and cash in US dollars. A valid card is needed to guarantee any booking, including ones made by phone at night — though we never take card numbers over the phone.

facts.question: What is your cancellation policy?
facts.answer: Free cancellation until 3:00 PM Pacific time on your arrival day. After that, or if you don't show, we charge the first night to the card on file.

facts.question: How do I get there from the airport?
facts.answer: From SFO, a taxi or rideshare takes 20 to 25 minutes and runs $40 to $60 — at 2 AM it's the only real option. By day, BART to Powell Street then a ten-minute Muni ride works, or Caltrain from Millbrae drops you three blocks from our door at 4th and King.

facts.question: Can you store our luggage?
facts.answer: Yes, free in a locked room beside the desk on your arrival and departure days. Plenty of guests leave bags after the 11:00 AM check-out and wander the waterfront until an evening train.

facts.question: Can I smoke in the room?
facts.answer: The whole building is non-smoking — San Francisco ordinance, and a $250 deep-cleaning fee if a room is smoked in. That includes the rooftop deck, I'm afraid.

facts.question: Do you accept children, and can we get an extra bed?
facts.answer: Children are very welcome. A crib for under-2s is free in any room, and the sofa bed in the Skyline Junior Suite sleeps a third guest at no extra charge.

facts.question: Is there a pool, gym or spa?
facts.answer: No pool or spa — the building's too lean for that. There's a compact 24-hour fitness room on the second floor, and the rooftop deck with fire tables does the unwinding instead.

facts.question: What's around the hotel?
facts.answer: We're in SoMa's old warehouse blocks: the Caltrain station is 4 minutes on foot, Oracle Park 10 minutes, the Embarcadero waterfront 15, and South Park's cafes 5. The blocks right around us are quiet at night — mostly lofts and offices.

facts.question: Is the area noisy at night?
facts.answer: Most nights it's very quiet — warehouses don't snore. On Giants game nights the foot traffic down Townsend gets loud until about 11:00 PM; lightwell-facing Compact Queens hear none of it, and we keep earplugs at the desk regardless.

facts.question: Is there a safe in the room?
facts.answer: Yes, every room has a laptop-sized electronic safe in the wardrobe, free to use.

facts.question: Do you offer laundry service?
facts.answer: Yes — drop a bag at the desk by 9:00 AM on a weekday and it's back by 7:00 PM the same day, priced per item; a shirt is $6. No service on Sundays.

facts.question: Are there any taxes on top of the room rate?
facts.answer: Yes — San Francisco adds a 14% transient occupancy tax plus about 2.25% in tourism district assessments at checkout. There's no resort fee here.

facts.question: What's the story of the building?
facts.answer: It was built in 1924 as a coffee warehouse and roasting plant serving the rail yards across Townsend. The conversion kept the timber columns, the freight arches and the old floor scale by the elevator — and the cafe downstairs still roasts twice a week, so the lobby smells like 1924 in the best way.

name: The Roundhouse Hotel
archetype: railroad-heritage hotel in a converted 1907 brick freight depot
short_description: A 28-room railroad-heritage hotel in a 1907 brick freight depot on Brannan Street, an eight-minute walk from the Caltrain station, with air conditioning and walk-in showers in every room and breakfast served in the old Dining Car room.
long_description: The Roundhouse Hotel occupies a 1907 brick freight depot on Brannan Street, built the year after the great earthquake to serve the rail yards that once filled this corner of South Beach. The hotel leans happily into its heritage: the lobby keeps a working semaphore signal and a wall of vintage timetables, the breakfast room is fitted out like a dining car, and the corridors are numbered like platforms. The 28 rooms are simple and exactly right for travelers off a late train — proper beds, individually controlled air conditioning, walk-in showers with real water pressure, blackout curtains, and outlets everywhere you'd want one. Breakfast is included and served from 6:30 AM, early enough for the first southbound departures. The Caltrain station at 4th and King is an eight-minute level walk; Oracle Park is five minutes, so book ahead on game nights. The desk — the staff call it the ticket window — is staffed from 7:00 AM to 11:00 PM, and through the night an AI receptionist answers the phone, checks live availability, and gets late arrivals through the door with codes sent by text.

policies.check_in_until: 11:00 PM at the staffed desk; self check-in with door codes after 11:00 PM
policies.cancellation: Free cancellation until 6:00 PM Pacific time on the day before arrival; after that the first night is charged. No-shows are charged the first night only.
policies.pets: Dogs up to 40 lbs welcome, one per room, $25 per stay; the Dining Car breakfast room is people-only but the lobby has a dog basket by the semaphore.
policies.smoking: Non-smoking throughout, per San Francisco ordinance; a $200 deep-cleaning fee applies if a room is smoked in. The nearest legal spot is the sidewalk, 20 feet from the door.
policies.payment_methods: Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, Apple Pay and Google Pay, and cash in US dollars; a valid card guarantees every booking.
policies.late_arrival_notes: The ticket window is staffed from 7:00 AM to 11:00 PM. After 11:00 PM the hotel's AI night receptionist handles arrivals: once a room is held under your name, it texts a one-time code for the Brannan Street door and a code for the brass key lockers beside the desk — they look like old station lockers, and your room key and breakfast card wait inside. An on-call manager lives eight minutes away and can be reached through this same line at any hour.

amenities: Breakfast included, served in the Dining Car room from 6:30 AM
Individually controlled air conditioning in all rooms
Walk-in showers with strong water pressure
Blackout curtains and quiet double-glazed windows
Free fiber Wi-Fi (about 400 Mbps) throughout
Working semaphore signal and vintage timetable wall in the lobby
24-hour coffee, tea and hot chocolate in the lobby
Free luggage storage on arrival and departure days
Laptop-sized in-room safes
Wash-and-fold laundry service ($15 per bag, weekdays)
Free earplugs at the desk for game nights

persona.greeting: Good evening — you've reached The Roundhouse Hotel in South Beach, right down the line from the Caltrain station. I'm the hotel's AI night receptionist; where can I get you tonight?
persona.speech_quirks: Talks like a fond old stationmaster: things run 'on time' or 'right on schedule', guests get 'rolling', and good news 'comes in on track one'. Always mentions how close the Caltrain station is when arrivals come up, and never rushes a caller — 'no hurry, the night desk doesn't close'.
persona.escalation_line: That one's above my pay grade at the night desk — let me write it on the morning board, and the day crew will call you back when they pull in at 7:00 AM sharp.

room_types.name: Standard Queen
room_types.description: The house workhorse: a queen bed under exposed 1907 brick, a walk-in shower with serious water pressure, air conditioning, and blackout curtains that keep the morning out until you're ready for it.

room_types.name: Pullman Twin
room_types.description: Two proper single beds in a room styled after a Pullman sleeper — reading lamps, polished wood headboards, and a walk-in shower. Popular with friends off the same late train.

room_types.name: Signal King
room_types.description: A larger king room on the Brannan side with tall depot windows, a small desk built from reclaimed boxcar planking, a walk-in rain shower, and air conditioning you control yourself.

room_types.name: Caboose Family Room
room_types.description: The big corner room at the end of the platform corridor: a queen bed plus two singles in a snug alcove, a walk-in shower, and a basket of wooden trains that has outlasted many bedtimes. Sleeps four.
features: Queen bed plus two single beds

facts.question: How far are you from the Caltrain station?
facts.answer: An eight-minute level walk — about four-tenths of a mile. Out of the Caltrain station at 4th and King, walk one block up 4th Street, turn right on Brannan, and we're two and a half blocks along on the left at number 340. Flat sidewalk the whole way, easy with a suitcase.

facts.question: What time is check-in?
facts.answer: Check-in is from 3:00 PM at the ticket window, which is staffed until 11:00 PM. After that the night procedure takes over — door codes by text, smooth as a timetable.

facts.question: When do I need to check out?
facts.answer: Check-out is by 11:00 AM. Late check-out until 1:00 PM is $25 when the room isn't re-let — ask the evening before and we'll pencil you in.

facts.question: I arrive around 2 AM — can I still check in?
facts.answer: Right on time as far as we're concerned. After 11:00 PM I confirm your booking and text you two codes: one for the Brannan Street door and one for the brass key lockers beside the desk, where your room key and breakfast card wait. An on-call manager is eight minutes away if anything jams.

facts.question: Do the rooms have air conditioning?
facts.answer: Yes, every room — individually controlled, fitted in the 2021 refit. Plenty of San Francisco hotels skip it; we didn't, and August guests thank us.

facts.question: Do the rooms have their own shower?
facts.answer: Every room has its own private bathroom with a walk-in shower — the water pressure is a point of pride, and the boiler was sized for a hotel twice our size.

facts.question: Is breakfast included?
facts.answer: Yes, with every rate — served in the Dining Car room from 6:30 to 10:00 AM: sourdough toast, eggs, fruit, yogurt, and bottomless coffee. We open early enough for the first southbound Caltrain departures.

facts.question: Do you have parking?
facts.answer: No garage of our own. The 24-hour garage at 255 King Street is a six-minute walk, about $42 overnight, and there's metered street parking on Brannan that's free overnight but fills on game nights.

facts.question: Can I bring my dog?
facts.answer: Dogs up to 40 lbs ride along happily — one per room, $25 per stay. The breakfast room is people-only, but there's a dog basket in the lobby by the semaphore signal.

facts.question: Is there Wi-Fi?
facts.answer: Free fiber Wi-Fi throughout at about 400 Mbps. The network is 'Roundhouse' and the password is on your key sleeve — or in your arrival text if you come in on the night shift.

facts.question: Is the hotel wheelchair accessible?
facts.answer: Yes — the depot is all heavy timber and wide doorways. There's a ramp at the Brannan entrance, an elevator to both floors, and two ADA Standard Queens with roll-in showers. Ask when booking and we'll hold one.

facts.question: What payment methods do you accept?
facts.answer: Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, Apple Pay, Google Pay, and US dollars in cash. A card guarantees the booking — but we never take card numbers over the phone; payment is settled at the hotel.

facts.question: What is your cancellation policy?
facts.answer: Free cancellation until 6:00 PM Pacific time the day before arrival. After that, or for a no-show, we charge the first night only.

facts.question: How do I get there from the airport?
facts.answer: From SFO at night, a taxi or rideshare runs $40 to $60 and takes about 20 minutes. By day, take BART to Millbrae and switch to Caltrain — it drops you at 4th and King, eight minutes from our door, which we consider the only civilized way to arrive.

facts.question: Can you store our luggage?
facts.answer: Free of charge on arrival and departure days, in the old parcel room behind the desk. Drop bags from 7:00 AM and collect until 11:00 PM — after that, ask me and I'll arrange a locker code.

facts.question: Can I smoke in the room?
facts.answer: No — the whole depot is non-smoking, with a $200 deep-cleaning fee if a room is smoked in. California law puts the nearest legal puff on the sidewalk, 20 feet from the door.

facts.question: Are children welcome, and can we get an extra bed?
facts.answer: Very welcome — the Caboose Family Room sleeps four and comes with a basket of wooden trains. A crib for under-2s is free in any room; we don't add extra beds elsewhere, the rooms are sized as they are.

facts.question: Is there a pool, gym or spa?
facts.answer: None of the three — we're a freight depot, not a resort. The Embarcadero waterfront is a flat ten-minute walk and makes a better gym anyway; ask the desk for the running map.

facts.question: What's the neighborhood like?
facts.answer: South Beach: the Caltrain station is 8 minutes' walk, Oracle Park 5, South Park's cafes 3, and the Embarcadero 10. By day it hums with offices and ballpark crowds; by midnight it's one of the quieter corners of the city.

facts.question: Is it noisy at night?
facts.answer: Quiet, with one scheduled exception: Giants game nights, when Brannan Street chatters until about 11:00 PM. Double glazing handles most of it, free earplugs at the desk handle the rest, and the Caboose corner room hears nothing at all.

facts.question: Is there a safe in the room?
facts.answer: Yes — a laptop-sized electronic safe in every room, free to use.

facts.question: Do you offer laundry service?
facts.answer: Wash-and-fold at $15 per bag: hand it over the ticket window by 9:00 AM on a weekday and it's back the same evening. There's also a self-service laundromat on Townsend, five minutes away.

facts.question: Are there any taxes or extra fees?
facts.answer: San Francisco adds a 14% transient occupancy tax plus about 2.25% in tourism assessments at checkout. No resort fee, no 'urban amenity fee', none of that.

facts.question: What's the story of the building?
facts.answer: It went up in 1907, the year after the earthquake and fire, as a brick freight depot for the rail yards that covered this end of South Beach. The semaphore in the lobby is a working original, the timetable wall is real paper from the 1920s through the 1950s, and the corridors are numbered like platforms because we couldn't resist.

facts.question: Is the hotel affected by baseball games?
facts.answer: Oracle Park is five minutes away, so on Giants home games the neighborhood fills from about 5:00 PM and rooms sell out early — book ahead. Upside: the walk to your seat beats any parking lot in the city.

name: Mission Bay Grand Hotel
archetype: modern glass conference hotel on the Mission Bay waterfront
address: 455 Mission Bay Boulevard North, San Francisco, CA 94158
short_description: A 142-room glass-and-steel conference hotel on the Mission Bay waterfront near Chase Center, with a heated rooftop lap pool, floor-to-ceiling bay views, and a 24-hour front desk — twelve minutes' walk from the Caltrain station.
long_description: The Mission Bay Grand rises eight glass stories over Mission Bay Boulevard, in the new quarter of biotech campuses and waterfront parks between the Caltrain station and Chase Center. Its 142 rooms are built for people who arrive late and leave early: floor-to-ceiling windows, individually controlled climate, rain showers, desks that actually fit a laptop and an elbow, and blackout shades that close at the touch of a button. The rooftop holds a heated 60-foot lap pool and a bar with a straight view across the water to the East Bay hills. Downstairs there's a grab-and-go market open round the clock, a restaurant serving until 10:00 PM, and meeting floors that hum whenever a convention is in town — which is often; on city-wide conference weeks the hotel sells out to the last room. The front desk is attended 24 hours a day, and overnight phone calls are answered by the hotel's AI night receptionist, which can check live availability, place holds, and brief the desk on every expected arrival by name. The Caltrain station at 4th and King is a twelve-minute walk up Fourth Street, across the Lefty O'Doul Bridge.

policies.cancellation: Free cancellation until 11:59 PM Pacific time two days before arrival; after that the first night is charged to the card on file. No-shows are charged the first night.
policies.pets: Dogs up to 50 lbs welcome on the designated pet floor for a $75 fee per stay, with bed and bowls provided. Service animals stay free of charge anywhere in the hotel.
policies.smoking: Entirely non-smoking, including balconies and the pool deck; a $300 deep-cleaning fee applies. The nearest smoking area is the plaza across the boulevard.
policies.payment_methods: Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, Diners Club, Apple Pay and Google Pay, and cash in US dollars. A valid credit card is required at booking to guarantee the reservation.
policies.late_arrival_notes: The front desk is attended 24 hours a day. For security the lobby doors lock between midnight and 5:00 AM; tap the intercom at the main entrance on Mission Bay Boulevard and the overnight agent will admit you and complete check-in in person at any hour. Reservations made overnight by phone are held all night under the guest's name — the desk is briefed on every expected arrival — and no payment details are ever taken by phone.

Rooftop bar with East Bay views (until 11:00 PM)
24-hour front desk and concierge
24-hour grab-and-go market

persona.style: polished, composed, briskly courteous, sincerely apologetic
persona.greeting: Good evening, thank you for calling the Mission Bay Grand — this is the hotel's AI night receptionist speaking; how may I assist you tonight?
persona.speech_quirks: Speaks in clean, efficient sentences with conference-hotel polish — 'absolutely', 'right away', 'let me confirm that for you' — and always repeats dates, names and totals back before acting. When the hotel is full she apologizes once, sincerely, and immediately pivots to what she CAN do: other nights, or pointing the caller toward the hotels closer to the Caltrain station.
persona.escalation_line: I want to make sure that's handled properly rather than quickly — may I take your name and number? Our guest services manager comes on at 7:00 AM and will call you back first thing.

room_types.name: Urban King
room_types.description: A 320-square-foot king room facing the city side, with floor-to-ceiling glass, motorized blackout shades, a rain shower, and a desk built for actual work. The hotel's standard, and its bestseller.

room_types.name: Double Queen
room_types.description: Two queen beds in 360 square feet on the courtyard side — the conference-week workhorse, and the family pick on weekends. Rain shower, climate control, and shades that black out the morning.

room_types.name: Club King
room_types.description: Upper-floor kings on the water side with a view across Mission Creek to the bay, lounge access with evening appetizers, and a bathroom with both soaking tub and rain shower.
features: Upper floor, bay and creek views

room_types.name: Bay Suite
room_types.description: Corner suites with a separate living room, dining table for four, wraparound water views toward the East Bay hills, and a sofa bed that brings capacity to four.
features: Corner living room with dining table

facts.question: Do you have a room available tonight?
facts.answer: I'm very sorry — tonight we are fully committed: a city-wide biotech convention and an event at Chase Center have taken all 142 rooms. I'm glad to check tomorrow or any other night, or to point you toward good hotels closer to the Caltrain station that may have rooms at this hour.

facts.question: How far are you from the Caltrain station?
facts.answer: A twelve-minute walk — about six-tenths of a mile. From the Caltrain station at 4th and King, head south on Fourth Street across the Lefty O'Doul drawbridge, and we're on Mission Bay Boulevard North just past the park. The T Third streetcar also covers it in one stop.

facts.question: What time can I check in?
facts.answer: Check-in begins at 3:00 PM, and the front desk is attended 24 hours, so there's no such thing as arriving too late. Early arrivals are welcome to store luggage and use the pool deck while they wait.

facts.question: What time is check-out?
facts.answer: Check-out is by 11:00 AM. Late check-out until 2:00 PM can be arranged for $60, subject to availability — on convention mornings it's tight, I won't pretend otherwise.

facts.question: I'll arrive around 2 AM — is that a problem?
facts.answer: Not at all — the desk is attended around the clock. Between midnight and 5:00 AM the lobby doors are locked for security: tap the intercom at the main entrance and the overnight agent will let you in and check you in personally. Any reservation held under your name is kept all night.

facts.question: Do the rooms have air conditioning?
facts.answer: Yes — every room has individually controlled heating and cooling, and the floor-to-ceiling glass is double-glazed, so the room holds whatever temperature you pick.

facts.question: Do you have parking?
facts.answer: Valet parking with in-and-out privileges is $72 per night, with EV chargers available. Self-parking at the public garage on 4th and Channel is about $38 overnight, a four-minute walk.

facts.question: What time is breakfast and how much does it cost?
facts.answer: Breakfast is served in the restaurant from 6:00 to 10:30 AM at $32 per person, or grab coffee and pastries at the 24-hour market downstairs any time. Club King and Bay Suite guests have breakfast included in the lounge.

facts.question: Can I bring my dog?
facts.answer: Dogs up to 50 lbs are welcome on our designated pet floor for a $75 fee per stay, with a bed and bowls waiting. Service animals stay free anywhere in the hotel.

facts.question: Is there Wi-Fi?
facts.answer: Complimentary fiber Wi-Fi covers the entire hotel including the pool deck. The network is 'MBGrand Guest'; the access code comes with check-in.

facts.question: Is the hotel wheelchair accessible?
facts.answer: Fully — step-free entrances, elevators to all floors, and twelve ADA rooms across every category, including roll-in showers and pool lift access. Request an accessible room at booking and it's guaranteed.

facts.question: What payment methods do you accept?
facts.answer: Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, Diners Club, Apple Pay, Google Pay, and cash in US dollars. A credit card guarantees the reservation; we never take card numbers by phone.

facts.question: What is your cancellation policy?
facts.answer: Free cancellation until 11:59 PM Pacific time two days before arrival. Inside that window, or for a no-show, the first night is charged to the card on file.

facts.question: How do I get to the hotel from the airport?
facts.answer: SFO is about 25 minutes by car; a taxi or rideshare runs $45 to $65 and is the right answer overnight. By day, BART to Millbrae connects to Caltrain, which arrives twelve minutes' walk away at 4th and King — or stay on the T Third streetcar to our corner.

facts.question: Can you store my luggage?
facts.answer: Of course — the bell desk stores luggage free of charge before check-in and after check-out, for as long as you need on the day of travel.

facts.question: Is smoking allowed?
facts.answer: The hotel is entirely non-smoking, including balconies and the pool deck, with a $300 cleaning fee for violations. The nearest smoking area is the plaza across the boulevard.

facts.question: Do children stay free, and can I get an extra bed?
facts.answer: Children under 18 stay free in existing bedding, cribs are free, and rollaway beds are $30 per night in Double Queens and suites. The pool has family hours from 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM.

facts.question: Is there a pool or gym?
facts.answer: The heated rooftop lap pool and hot tub are open 7:00 AM to 10:00 PM, and the fitness center — Pelotons, weights, rowers — is open 24 hours with your key card. No spa, but in-room massage can be booked a day ahead.

facts.question: What is the area around the hotel like?
facts.answer: Mission Bay: new, calm, and walkable — Chase Center is 5 minutes away, the waterfront parks 2, the UCSF campus across the boulevard, and the Caltrain station 12 minutes up Fourth Street. Restaurants cluster around the arena and along the creek.

facts.question: Is the hotel noisy at night?
facts.answer: It's a quiet neighborhood at night. On Chase Center event nights the plaza buzzes until around 11:30 PM — courtyard-side Double Queens hear none of it, and the glazing keeps water-side rooms calm too.

facts.question: Is there a safe in the room?
facts.answer: Every room has a laptop-sized electronic safe, and the front desk has safe-deposit boxes for anything larger.

facts.question: Do you offer laundry service?
facts.answer: Laundry and pressing collected before 9:00 AM comes back the same day by 6:00 PM on weekdays; a shirt is $9. There's also a guest launderette on the second floor, $4 per machine.

facts.question: Are taxes included in the room rate?
facts.answer: Quoted rates exclude tax — San Francisco adds a 14% transient occupancy tax plus about 2.25% in tourism district assessments at checkout. We do not charge a resort or destination fee.

facts.question: How close are you to Chase Center?
facts.answer: Five minutes on foot across the plaza — close enough to hear the crowd roar from the rooftop bar on game nights. On event dates we sell out weeks ahead, so book early or aim for a non-event night.

facts.question: Why are you sold out so often?
facts.answer: Mission Bay sits between the UCSF campus, the biotech district and Chase Center, so city-wide conventions and arena events can take the whole house at once. Tonight is one of those nights, I'm afraid — every one of our 142 rooms is spoken for.

name: The Belgrave San Francisco
archetype: five-star grand dame hotel, marble lobby, since 1911
address: 79 New Montgomery Street, San Francisco, CA 94105
short_description: San Francisco's grand dame since 1911 on New Montgomery Street: 96 rooms and suites behind a Tennessee-marble lobby, with white-glove service, a Palm Court for afternoon tea, an indoor pool, and a 24-hour attended desk.
long_description: The Belgrave has stood on New Montgomery Street since 1911, raised in the great rebuilding after the earthquake and fire, and has never closed its brass doors since. The lobby remains as it was built — Tennessee marble underfoot, a birdcage elevator still operated on request, and the original guest ledger displayed at the concierge desk. Behind the Beaux-Arts facade, the 96 rooms and suites were quietly renovated in 2023 with triple glazing, individually controlled climate, and marble bathrooms, while the Palm Court still pours afternoon tea beneath its leaded-glass dome as it has since the 1920s. Guests dine at The Montgomery Room, swim in the columned indoor pool below street level, and step out to a doorman who has an umbrella open before the fog turns to drizzle. Reception is attended around the clock — a tradition the house keeps even at four in the morning — and overnight telephone calls are answered by the hotel's AI night receptionist, which holds rooms by name and briefs the night concierge on every expected arrival. The Caltrain station at 4th and King is about a mile away: an eighteen-minute walk down Second Street, or eight minutes by taxi.

policies.cancellation: Free cancellation until 3:00 PM Pacific time two days before arrival; after that the first night is charged to the card on file. No-shows are charged the first night.
policies.pets: Dogs up to 25 lbs are welcome at $95 per stay, one per room, with bed, bowls and a turndown biscuit provided. Service animals stay free of charge.
policies.smoking: Entirely non-smoking indoors, per San Francisco ordinance; smoking in a room incurs a $400 deep-cleaning fee. The doorman can direct guests to the courtyard smoking area.
policies.payment_methods: Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, Diners Club, Apple Pay, Google Pay and cash in US dollars. A valid credit card is required at the time of booking to guarantee the reservation.
policies.late_arrival_notes: Reception is attended 24 hours a day. Between midnight and 5:30 AM the brass front doors on New Montgomery Street are locked for security; ring the night bell at the main entrance and the night concierge will admit you and complete check-in personally at any hour. Reservations made overnight by telephone are held all night under the guest's name — the night concierge is notified of every expected arrival — and no payment details are ever taken by phone.

amenities: 24-hour attended reception and concierge
The Montgomery Room (California fine dining)
Palm Court afternoon tea under a leaded-glass dome
Columned indoor pool and steam room
Spa with four treatment rooms
24-hour fitness center
Valet parking ($78 per night, in-and-out)
24-hour room service
Same-day laundry and pressing
Chauffeured house car within downtown (first come, first served)
Complimentary fiber Wi-Fi
Original 1911 birdcage elevator, operated on request
Babysitting on request
Three salons for events up to 120 guests

persona.greeting: Good evening, you have reached The Belgrave on New Montgomery Street — I am the hotel's AI night receptionist, at your service; how may I be of assistance?
persona.speech_quirks: Speaks in measured, unhurried sentences with classic courtesies — 'very good', 'of course', 'it would be our pleasure' — and never uses slang. Always repeats dates, names and amounts back to the caller for confirmation before proceeding, in the manner of a seasoned concierge.
persona.escalation_line: I do apologise — that is a matter I am not able to resolve on the hotel's behalf tonight. With your permission, I shall take a detailed message, and our guest relations team will telephone you after seven o'clock this morning.

room_types.name: Classic Queen
room_types.description: A 300-square-foot room over the inner courtyard or the quiet side street, with the hotel's signature Calacatta-marble bathroom, a walk-in rain shower, and understated 1910s-inspired furnishings.
features: Marble bathroom with walk-in rain shower

room_types.name: Deluxe King
room_types.description: A 380-square-foot king room facing New Montgomery Street, with a marble bathroom holding both soaking tub and rain shower, a seating area with writing desk, and nightly turndown.

room_types.name: Junior Suite
room_types.description: A 520-square-foot suite with a separate sitting area and sofa bed for a third guest, choice of street or courtyard aspect, and a deep soaking tub beside a separate rain shower.
features: Soaking tub and separate rain shower

room_types.name: Belgrave Suite
room_types.description: The 800-square-foot corner suite on the ninth floor, with bay windows up New Montgomery toward Market Street, original 1911 parquet, a dining table for four, and two marble bathrooms.

facts.question: How far are you from the Caltrain station?
facts.answer: About a mile — an eighteen-minute walk from the Caltrain station at 4th and King, straight down Second Street and right on New Montgomery. At night we would suggest a taxi: eight minutes and roughly $12 to $15, and the doorman will be expecting you.

facts.question: What time can I check in?
facts.answer: Check-in begins at 3:00 PM. Should you arrive earlier, the concierge will store your luggage with pleasure, and you are welcome to take coffee in the Palm Court or use the pool while you wait.

facts.question: What time is check-out?
facts.answer: Check-out is by 12:00 noon. Late check-out until 3:00 PM can be arranged for $120, subject to availability on the day.

facts.question: I'll arrive around 2 AM — is that a problem?
facts.answer: Not at all — reception is attended 24 hours. Between midnight and 5:30 AM the front doors are locked for security; simply ring the night bell at the main entrance on New Montgomery Street and the night concierge will admit you and complete your check-in personally.

facts.question: If I book now in the middle of the night, is the room guaranteed?
facts.answer: Yes — a room held under your name overnight is kept for the entire night, and the night concierge is informed of every expected arrival by name. You will receive a text confirmation immediately after booking; payment is settled at the hotel, never by phone.

facts.question: Do the rooms have air conditioning?
facts.answer: Yes — every room has individually controlled heating and cooling, discreetly retrofitted in the 2023 renovation, and the windows still open should you prefer the sea air.

facts.question: Do you have parking?
facts.answer: We offer valet parking for $78 per night with unlimited in-and-out privileges. Pull up directly at the entrance and the doorman will take your car; electric vehicle charging is available on request.

facts.question: What time is breakfast and how much does it cost?
facts.answer: Breakfast is served in The Montgomery Room from 6:30 to 10:30 AM, until 11:00 AM on weekends, at $42 per person when not included in your rate.

facts.question: Can I bring my dog?
facts.answer: Dogs up to 25 lbs are welcome at $95 per stay, one per room, with a bed, bowls and a turndown biscuit provided. Service animals of any size stay free of charge.

facts.question: Is there Wi-Fi?
facts.answer: Complimentary fiber Wi-Fi covers the entire hotel. The network is 'Belgrave Guest' and the password is provided at check-in.

facts.question: Is the hotel wheelchair accessible?
facts.answer: Yes — despite the 1911 building, we have a step-free entrance via the ramp at the side door, elevators to all floors, and five accessible rooms with roll-in showers. Please request an accessible room when booking.

facts.question: What payment methods do you accept?
facts.answer: We accept Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, Diners Club, Apple Pay, Google Pay and cash in US dollars. A valid credit card is required to guarantee any reservation; we never take card numbers by telephone.

facts.question: What is your cancellation policy?
facts.answer: Cancellation is free of charge until 3:00 PM Pacific time two days before arrival. After that, or in case of no-show, the first night is charged to the card on file.

facts.question: How do I get to the hotel from the airport?
facts.answer: SFO is about 20 minutes by car. Our chauffeured house car can collect you for $95 each way at any hour, including overnight; a metered taxi is roughly $55. By day, BART runs from the airport to Montgomery Street station, three minutes' walk from our door.

facts.question: Can you store my luggage?
facts.answer: Certainly — the concierge stores luggage free of charge, both before check-in and after check-out, for as long as you need on the day of travel.

facts.question: Is smoking allowed?
facts.answer: The hotel is entirely non-smoking indoors, and smoking in a room incurs a $400 cleaning fee. The doorman will gladly direct you to the courtyard smoking area.

facts.question: Do children stay free, and can I get an extra bed?
facts.answer: Children under 12 stay free in existing bedding, and a crib is provided at no charge. A rollaway bed is $75 per night and is available in Deluxe Kings and the suites.

facts.question: Is there a pool or spa?
facts.answer: The columned indoor pool and steam room are open from 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM, and the fitness center is open 24 hours with your room key. Spa treatments are best reserved a day in advance.

facts.question: What is the area around the hotel like?
facts.answer: We stand on New Montgomery Street at the edge of Yerba Buena: SFMOMA is three minutes on foot, Market Street two, the Yerba Buena Gardens five, and Union Square ten. The Montgomery BART and Muni station is three minutes from the door.

facts.question: Is the hotel noisy at night?
facts.answer: Rooms facing New Montgomery were fitted with triple glazing in 2023 and are very quiet. For absolute silence we recommend a Classic Queen overlooking the interior courtyard.

facts.question: Is there a safe in the room?
facts.answer: Every room has a laptop-sized electronic safe. For larger valuables, the reception safe-deposit boxes — in service since 1911 — are at your disposal free of charge.

facts.question: Do you offer laundry service?
facts.answer: Laundry collected before 9:00 AM is returned the same day by 7:00 PM; a shirt is $14 and dry-cleaning a suit is $36. Express four-hour pressing is also available.

facts.question: Are taxes included in the room rate?
facts.answer: Quoted rates exclude tax — San Francisco adds a 14% transient occupancy tax plus approximately 2.25% in tourism district assessments at checkout. The Belgrave has never charged a resort fee and does not intend to begin.

facts.question: Has the hotel really been open since 1911?
facts.answer: Indeed — we opened in October 1911, raised in the great rebuilding after the earthquake and fire, and have never closed. The Tennessee-marble lobby, the birdcage elevator and the guest ledger at the concierge desk are all original, and the elevator is still operated for guests on request.

facts.question: Do you serve afternoon tea?
facts.answer: Afternoon tea is served daily in the Palm Court from 2:00 to 5:00 PM at $58 per person — a house tradition since the 1920s, beneath the leaded-glass dome, with warm scones and a glass of California sparkling to finish.

facts.question: Is there a dress code at the restaurant?
facts.answer: We ask for smart-casual attire in The Montgomery Room after 6:00 PM — no sportswear, please. Elsewhere in the hotel, no dress code applies.

name: Harbor House Guesthouse
short_description: A 12-room family guesthouse on a quiet SoMa side street, run by the Moreno family since 1962. Simple, spotless rooms — some with private bathrooms, some sharing in the old boarding-house tradition — homemade pan dulce at breakfast, and the Caltrain station six minutes' walk away.
long_description: Harbor House has been taking in travelers on Ritch Street since 1962, when Gus Moreno hung the anchor sign by the door and let rooms to longshoremen and railroad men between shifts. Today his granddaughter Lupe runs the house the same way: twelve simple rooms over three floors of a tall 1908 boarding house, fresh pan dulce from the family's bakery tray every morning, and a handwritten list of taquerias and late-night diners pressed into the hand of anyone who asks where to eat. Rooms are modest but immaculate — four with private bathrooms and showers, the rest sharing two scrubbed bathrooms per corridor in the old tradition — cooled by ceiling fans and the evening fog, since the house has never needed air conditioning. There is no elevator and there are 38 stairs, but in exchange you sleep six minutes from the Caltrain station at 4th and King for the price of a parking spot at the hotels downtown. The family staffs the desk from 7:00 AM to 10:00 PM; overnight, an AI receptionist answers the phone and handles late arrivals with door codes sent by text.

policies.check_in_until: 10:00 PM at the staffed desk; self check-in with door codes after 10:00 PM
policies.cancellation: Free cancellation until 6:00 PM Pacific time on the day before arrival; after that the first night is charged. No-shows are charged the first night only.
policies.pets: Small dogs up to 20 lbs are welcome free of charge by prior arrangement, in the ensuite double and family rooms only.
policies.smoking: Non-smoking throughout the house, per San Francisco ordinance; a $100 deep-cleaning fee applies if a room smells of smoke. Guests smoke on the sidewalk.
policies.payment_methods: Cash (US dollars), Visa, Mastercard and debit cards. No American Express. Night arrivals settle the first night by a secure card link sent by text — never by reading numbers over the phone.
policies.late_arrival_notes: The desk is staffed by the family from 7:00 AM to 10:00 PM. Between 10:00 PM and 6:00 AM the AI night receptionist handles arrivals: once the first night is settled via the secure card link sent by text, it sends two codes — the street-door code for 108 Ritch Street and the key-safe code for the box beside the front desk. Room keys wait inside the key safe; Lupe greets late arrivals personally at breakfast.

amenities: Free Wi-Fi throughout
Continental breakfast included (7:00-10:00 AM) with homemade pan dulce
Sunny breakfast room with the original 1962 counter
Free luggage storage on arrival and departure days
In-room safes
Daily housekeeping
Wash-and-fold laundry service ($12 per load)
Ceiling fans in every room (the fog does the rest)
Tea and coffee corner at the desk
Handwritten neighborhood food list (the 'lista de Lupe')
Free earplugs and city maps at the desk
Baby cribs free of charge; folding extra beds $15

persona.greeting: Good evening, mijo — you've reached Harbor House on Ritch Street, and I'm the family's AI night receptionist; how can I help you?
persona.speech_quirks: Speaks gently and unhurriedly, calls callers 'dear' or 'mijo' — 'that's like saying sweetheart', she explains. Apologizes warmly and often, fusses over callers' wellbeing — asks if they've eaten, warns about the 38 stairs — and cannot resist recommending the family's favorite taquerias even when she has nothing else to offer.
persona.escalation_line: Ay, I'm so sorry, dear — that's something only the family can sort out. Let me take your name and number, and Lupe will call you back first thing in the morning, just after seven.

room_types.name: Single Room (shared bathroom)
room_types.description: A snug single on the second floor in the old boarding-house tradition: washbasin in the room, crisp linens, and a shared bathroom two doors down the corridor that is cleaned three times a day.

room_types.name: Twin Room (shared bathroom)
room_types.description: Two single beds, tall sash windows and a washbasin in the room; the shared bathroom is on the same corridor. Back-facing units look onto the quiet rear lightwell.

room_types.name: Double Room with private bathroom
room_types.description: The house's most requested room: a double bed, a compact private bathroom with shower, a ceiling fan, and on the street side a bay window over Ritch Street.

room_types.name: Family Room
room_types.description: The big corner room on the third floor with a double bed and two singles, a private bathroom with shower, and — if you lean by the window — a sliver of the Bay Bridge between the rooftops.
features: one double bed and two single beds
Bay Bridge glimpse between the rooftops

facts.question: How far are you from the Caltrain station?
facts.answer: Six minutes on foot, dear — out of the station at 4th and King, one block up Townsend, left on Ritch Street, and we're the tall green house with the anchor sign at number 108. Flat all the way, I promise.

facts.question: What time is check-in?
facts.answer: Check-in is from 2:00 PM to 10:00 PM at our little desk on the ground floor. If you'll arrive later than 10:00 PM, don't worry, dear — we arrange self check-in with door codes sent by text.

facts.question: When do I need to check out?
facts.answer: Check-out is by 11:00 AM. If your train is later, you're welcome to leave your bags with us for the day at no charge.

facts.question: I land after midnight — can I still check in at 2 AM?
facts.answer: Of course, mijo. Between 10:00 PM and 6:00 AM the AI night receptionist confirms your booking, settles the first night by a secure card link sent to your phone, and then texts two codes: one for the street door at 108 Ritch Street and one for the key safe beside the desk, where your room key is waiting.

facts.question: Do the rooms have air conditioning?
facts.answer: No air conditioning, dear — in sixty years the house has never needed it. Every room has a ceiling fan, the sash windows open, and the fog rolls in most evenings like clockwork to cool the city down.

facts.question: Do the rooms have their own bathroom?
facts.answer: Four of our rooms do — the ensuite doubles and the family room have their own bathroom with a shower. The singles and twins share two big spotless bathrooms on the corridor, cleaned three times a day, in the old boarding-house way.

facts.question: Do you have parking?
facts.answer: I'm sorry, we have none of our own — Ritch Street is narrow. The 24-hour garage at 255 King Street is about a seven-minute walk, roughly $42 overnight, and there's sometimes street parking on Townsend after 6:00 PM.

facts.question: Is breakfast included?
facts.answer: Yes, breakfast is included and served from 7:00 to 10:00 AM at the original 1962 counter: Lupe's pan dulce, fresh fruit, hard-boiled eggs, yogurt and proper coffee. Eat something, dear, the day is long.

facts.question: Can I bring my dog?
facts.answer: Small, well-behaved dogs up to about 20 lbs are welcome at no extra charge, but only in the ensuite double and family rooms, and we do ask you to arrange it with us in advance.

facts.question: Do you have Wi-Fi?
facts.answer: Yes, free Wi-Fi covers the whole house. The network is HarborGuest and the password is on the little card on your bedside table.

facts.question: Is the guesthouse wheelchair accessible?
facts.answer: I'm so sorry, dear — we're an old 1908 house with no elevator and 38 stairs to the rooms, so sadly we're not suitable for wheelchair users or anyone who struggles with stairs.

facts.question: What payment methods do you accept?
facts.answer: We take cash in US dollars, Visa, Mastercard and debit cards; we don't accept American Express, forgive us. For night arrivals, the first night is settled through a secure card link sent by text — we never ask for card numbers over the phone.

facts.question: What is your cancellation policy?
facts.answer: Cancellation is free until 6:00 PM Pacific time on the day before arrival; after that we charge the first night. No-shows are also charged the first night only.

facts.question: How do I get there from the airport?
facts.answer: From SFO the simplest by day is BART to Millbrae and Caltrain to 4th and King — about 45 minutes and $12, and then we're six minutes on foot. At night a taxi or rideshare takes 20 minutes and costs $40 to $55. Have you eaten? The taqueria on Townsend is open till 2:00 AM.

facts.question: Can you store my luggage?
facts.answer: Yes, free of charge on your arrival and departure days, in the locked cupboard behind the desk. The desk is staffed from 7:00 AM to 10:00 PM for dropping off and collecting bags.

facts.question: Can I smoke in the room?
facts.answer: I'm afraid the whole house is non-smoking — California law and house rule both — and there's a $100 cleaning charge if a room smells of smoke. Guests just step out to the sidewalk, it's only one flight down, dear.

facts.question: Can we add an extra bed for a child?
facts.answer: Children are very welcome. A folding extra bed is $15 per night and a baby crib is free — both fit only in the ensuite double and family rooms, and we ask you to request them a day ahead.

facts.question: Is there a pool, gym or spa?
facts.answer: No, dear, we're a small family house — no pool, gym or spa. But the Embarcadero is a fifteen-minute walk and Lupe always says the stairs are exercise enough.

facts.question: What is the neighborhood like?
facts.answer: We're on Ritch Street, a quiet little block in SoMa: the Caltrain station is 6 minutes' walk, South Park 3, Oracle Park 8, and the good taquerias of Townsend Street 2. Even when we're full I always tell people — stay near the station if you can, you'll walk everywhere.

facts.question: Is it noisy at night?
facts.answer: Our street is much quieter than the big SoMa avenues, dear. On Giants game nights you'll hear happy crowds until about 11:00 PM; the back rooms face the lightwell and hear almost nothing, and there are free earplugs at the desk just in case.

facts.question: Is there a safe in the room?
facts.answer: Yes, every room has a small digital safe in the wardrobe — big enough for passports, phones and a tablet. Anything larger can be locked in the main safe at the desk.

facts.question: Do you offer laundry service?
facts.answer: We do a wash-and-fold service for $12 per load — leave it at the desk by 10:00 AM and it's back the next morning. There's also a self-service laundromat on Brannan, about 4 minutes' walk.

facts.question: Can I check in early?
facts.answer: If your room is ready we'll let you in early at no charge, and you can leave bags with us from 7:00 AM either way. A guaranteed early check-in from 11:00 AM costs $20 and needs to be booked the day before.

facts.question: Are there any taxes or extra fees?
facts.answer: San Francisco adds a 14% transient occupancy tax plus about 2.25% in tourism assessments, which we collect at the house. No other fees, dear — Gus didn't believe in them and neither do we.

facts.question: Who runs the guesthouse?
facts.answer: The same family for three generations: Lupe Moreno runs it today, just as her grandfather Gus did when he opened the house in 1962 for longshoremen and railroad men. Gus's anchor sign still hangs by the door, repainted every spring.

facts.question: Do you serve dinner or recommend somewhere to eat?
facts.answer: We don't serve dinner, but ask at the desk for the 'lista de Lupe' — her handwritten list of family-run spots. Her favorite is the taqueria on Townsend two blocks down, open till 2:00 AM, where guests who mention us get free chips.

name: South Park Inn
archetype: Edwardian bed and breakfast on the old South Park oval
address: 15 South Park Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94107
short_description: An eleven-room Edwardian bed and breakfast from 1907 on the South Park oval, nine minutes' walk from the Caltrain station — bay windows, claw-foot charm, ensuite showers, full breakfast by the fire, and famously no air conditioning, because the fog does it better.
long_description: South Park Inn occupies a tall Edwardian house built in 1907 on the oval green of South Park — the oldest planned square in San Francisco, laid out in the 1850s in imitation of a London crescent. Inside it is exactly what the facade promises: eleven rooms with bay windows over the park or the quiet rear garden, brass beds, claw-foot tubs in two of the suites, and ensuite bathrooms with good showers throughout. What it does not have, and cheerfully refuses to apologize for, is air conditioning: the house sits in the kindest corner of the fog belt, every room has a ceiling fan and windows that open onto the green, and on the four genuinely hot days a year the innkeeper hands out iced tea and box fans. Breakfast is cooked — sourdough French toast is the house signature — and served by the fire from 7:30 AM. Jack London was born two blocks away, a fact the bookshelves never let guests forget. The innkeeper is in until 10:00 PM; overnight, an AI receptionist answers the phone, and late arrivals get a porch lockbox code by text.

policies.check_in_until: 10:00 PM at the desk; porch lockbox codes after 10:00 PM
policies.cancellation: Free cancellation until 3:00 PM Pacific time on the day before arrival; after that the first night is charged to the card on file. No-shows are charged the first night.
policies.pets: One dog of any polite size is welcome in the two garden-level rooms for $30 per stay; the park outside is effectively the world's best dog amenity.
policies.smoking: Non-smoking throughout, per San Francisco ordinance; a $150 deep-cleaning fee applies. The park bench across the street is traditional.
policies.payment_methods: Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, Apple Pay and cash in US dollars; a valid card is required to guarantee every booking.
policies.late_arrival_notes: The innkeeper is at the desk until 10:00 PM. After 10:00 PM the inn's AI night receptionist handles arrivals: once a room is held under your name, it texts the code for the lockbox on the porch at 15 South Park Avenue — your key, a map, and the Wi-Fi card wait inside — plus the front-door code. The innkeeper lives on the garden level and can be woken through this phone line for genuine emergencies.

amenities: Full cooked breakfast included (7:30-10:00 AM), sourdough French toast a specialty
Bay windows over the South Park oval
Ensuite bathrooms with showers in every room
Ceiling fans and opening windows (no air conditioning, by conviction)
Library parlor with fireplace and 1,200 books
Free fiber Wi-Fi throughout
Sherry hour by the fire, 5:00-6:00 PM daily
Free luggage storage on arrival and departure days
In-room safes
Garden patio with lemon tree
Loaner umbrellas (this is still San Francisco)

persona.greeting: Good evening, you've reached the South Park Inn, on the old oval — I'm the inn's AI night receptionist, minding the desk while the house sleeps; how can I help you?
persona.speech_quirks: Speaks like a well-read innkeeper: fond of the fog ('our natural air conditioning, on duty three hundred nights a year'), quick with a local fact — Jack London born two blocks east, the park laid out in 1855 — and honest to a fault about what the old house does and doesn't have. Recommends books the way other hotels recommend restaurants.
persona.escalation_line: That's one for the innkeeper herself, I'm afraid — let me note it in the morning book, and she'll call you back over her first coffee, a little after 7:00 AM.

room_types.name: Park Queen
room_types.description: A queen room with a bay window over the South Park oval, a brass bed, a ceiling fan, and an ensuite bathroom with a good strong shower. The morning light through the plane trees is the whole point.

room_types.name: Garden Double
room_types.description: Quiet doubles at the back of the house facing the garden and the lemon tree — the rooms the light sleepers ask for — with ensuite showers and a private patio door on the ground floor.
features: Ground-floor rooms open to the patio

room_types.name: Garret Suite
room_types.description: The two top-floor suites under the gables: a queen bed, a reading nook with a daybed that sleeps a third guest, a claw-foot tub beside the shower, and rooftop views toward the Bay Bridge.

facts.question: How far are you from the Caltrain station?
facts.answer: Nine minutes on foot — out of the Caltrain station at 4th and King, up Fourth Street two blocks, right on Bryant for one, then left into South Park; we're at number 15 on the oval, behind the plane trees. Flat the whole way.

facts.question: Do the rooms have air conditioning?
facts.answer: No — and we say so proudly before anyone books. The house has never had air conditioning: South Park sits in the gentlest corner of the fog belt, every room has a ceiling fan and windows that open onto the green, and on the rare hot day we hand out iced tea and box fans. If you truly need machine-made cold air, we'll cheerfully point you to the modern hotels by the station.

facts.question: Do the rooms have their own shower?
facts.answer: Yes — every room has its own ensuite bathroom with a proper shower, and the two Garret Suites add claw-foot tubs. The plumbing is newer than the house by a century, thankfully.

facts.question: What time is check-in?
facts.answer: Check-in runs from 3:00 to 10:00 PM at the desk in the parlor. Later than that is no trouble — the night procedure with the porch lockbox takes over, and I can set it up on this call.

facts.question: When do I need to check out?
facts.answer: Check-out is by 11:00 AM. Late check-out until 1:00 PM is $20 when the room isn't re-let — ask the evening before.

facts.question: I arrive around 2 AM — can I still check in?
facts.answer: Certainly — after 10:00 PM I confirm your booking and text you the front-door code plus the code for the lockbox on the porch, where your key, a map and the Wi-Fi card wait. The innkeeper sleeps on the garden level and can be woken through this line for genuine emergencies.

facts.question: Is breakfast included?
facts.answer: Yes, a full cooked breakfast by the fire, 7:30 to 10:00 AM — the sourdough French toast with whipped honey butter is the house signature, and there are eggs, fruit and bottomless coffee besides.

facts.question: Do you have parking?
facts.answer: No parking of our own — the park oval predates the automobile by half a century. The garage at 255 King Street is an eight-minute walk, about $42 overnight, and street spots on Bryant open up after 6:00 PM if you're lucky.

facts.question: Can I bring my dog?
facts.answer: One polite dog is welcome in the two garden-level rooms for $30 per stay — and the park outside the door is the best dog amenity in the city. We ask that they skip the breakfast parlor.

facts.question: Is there Wi-Fi?
facts.answer: Yes, free fiber Wi-Fi through the whole house — surprisingly quick for a building from 1907. The network is 'SouthParkInn'; the password is on the card in your room and in your arrival text.

facts.question: Is the inn wheelchair accessible?
facts.answer: Honestly, only partly — there are four steps up to the porch and no elevator. The two garden-level doubles are reachable by a ramp through the patio gate and have step-in showers, but the upper floors are stairs only. Call ahead and we'll talk it through properly.

facts.question: What payment methods do you accept?
facts.answer: Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, Apple Pay, and cash in US dollars. A card guarantees the booking; payment is settled at the inn, never over the phone.

facts.question: What is your cancellation policy?
facts.answer: Free cancellation until 3:00 PM Pacific time the day before arrival. After that, or for a no-show, the first night is charged to the card on file.

facts.question: How do I get there from the airport?
facts.answer: From SFO, a taxi or rideshare takes about 20 minutes and runs $40 to $55 — at night that's the answer. By day, BART to Millbrae then Caltrain to 4th and King leaves you a nine-minute walk through the old warehouse blocks.

facts.question: Can I leave my bags before check-in or after check-out?
facts.answer: Yes — free storage in the cedar closet off the parlor, on both arrival and departure days, available while the desk is staffed until 10:00 PM.

facts.question: Can I smoke in the room?
facts.answer: No — the house is non-smoking throughout, with a $150 cleaning fee, and it's an 1907 wooden building so we take it seriously. The park bench across the street is the traditional spot.

facts.question: Are children welcome, and can we get an extra bed?
facts.answer: Children are welcome — the Garret Suites sleep three with the daybed, and a crib is free for under-2s. The house is old and creaky and full of books, which children either love completely or not at all.

facts.question: Is there a pool, gym or spa?
facts.answer: Goodness, no — we have a fireplace, twelve hundred books, and a lemon tree. The Embarcadero makes a fine running track ten minutes away, and there's a day-pass gym on Townsend.

facts.question: What's the neighborhood like?
facts.answer: South Park is a hidden oval green ringed by cafes and old houses — laid out in 1855, the oldest planned square in the city. The Caltrain station is 9 minutes' walk, Oracle Park 8, South Park Cafe is next door, and Jack London's birthplace marker is two blocks east.

facts.question: Is it noisy at night?
facts.answer: The park is wonderfully quiet at night — the loudest thing is usually the foghorn on the bay, which most guests come to love. Game nights bring cheerful noise to Bryant Street until about 11:00 PM; the Garden Doubles at the back hear none of it.

facts.question: Is there a safe in the room?
facts.answer: Yes, every room has a small electronic safe in the wardrobe, free to use.

facts.question: Do you offer laundry service?
facts.answer: We don't do laundry in-house, but the wash-and-fold on Brannan Street collects from us on weekday mornings if you leave a bag at the desk by 9:00 AM — $18 a bag, back the same evening.

facts.question: Are there any taxes or extra fees?
facts.answer: San Francisco adds a 14% transient occupancy tax plus about 2.25% in tourism assessments at checkout. No resort fees — we're an inn, not a resort, and proud of the distinction.

facts.question: Is the building historic?
facts.answer: The house went up in 1907 during the great rebuilding, on the oval of South Park — laid out in 1855 in imitation of a London crescent, the oldest planned square in San Francisco. Jack London was born two blocks east in 1876; the parlor shelves carry everything he wrote, and guests keep stealing The Call of the Wild, which we consider fair.

facts.question: I heard you serve sherry in the afternoon — is that true?
facts.answer: Every day from 5:00 to 6:00 PM, by the parlor fire — sherry, or tea if you prefer, and whatever the innkeeper baked that morning. It's the hour the house is at its best, if you ask me.

name: Wharfside Anchor Hotel
archetype: cheerful tourist hotel a block from Fisherman's Wharf
short_description: A 70-room tourist hotel one block from the Fisherman's Wharf waterfront, with air conditioning and rain showers in every room, a sourdough-and-crab breakfast cafe, and the sea lions of Pier 39 ten minutes' stroll away — but a 20-minute cab ride from the Caltrain station.
long_description: The Wharfside Anchor sits on Jones Street one block back from the Fisherman's Wharf waterfront, close enough that on quiet nights the sea lions at Pier 39 provide the lullaby. Its 70 rooms were rebuilt in 2022 around the things vacationing families actually want: air conditioning in every room, rain showers, beds that forgive a day of hills, connecting-room options, and blackout curtains against the morning fog-glare. The cafe downstairs bakes sourdough every morning and does a crab roll at lunch that the staff are honestly a little smug about. Alcatraz ferries leave from Pier 33, twelve minutes on foot; the Powell-Hyde cable car turnaround is seven; Ghirardelli Square is five. The one thing the hotel cannot offer is the south side of the city — the Caltrain station at 4th and King is three miles away, a 50-minute walk or a 15-to-20-minute taxi, and the night desk says so honestly whenever a caller is starting from the station. Reception is staffed 24 hours; overnight phone calls are answered by the hotel's AI night receptionist.

policies.cancellation: Free cancellation until 3:00 PM Pacific time on the day before arrival; after that the first night is charged to the card on file. No-shows are charged the first night.
policies.pets: Dogs up to 40 lbs welcome for $40 per stay, one per room; the waterfront walk is a dog's idea of heaven, the breakfast cafe is people-only.
policies.smoking: Non-smoking throughout, per San Francisco ordinance; a $250 deep-cleaning fee applies. The nearest smoking area is the courtyard off the parking level.
policies.payment_methods: Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, Apple Pay and Google Pay, and cash in US dollars; a valid card guarantees every booking.
policies.late_arrival_notes: The front desk is staffed 24 hours a day. Between midnight and 5:00 AM the Jones Street doors are locked for security; press the buzzer at the entrance and the overnight clerk will let you in and complete check-in at the desk. Rooms held by phone overnight are kept all night under the guest's name, and no payment details are ever taken by phone.

amenities: Air conditioning in all rooms (2022 refit)
Rain showers and blackout curtains
24-hour front desk
Sourdough bakery cafe (breakfast and lunch)
Heated outdoor courtyard pool (seasonal, 9:00 AM-8:00 PM)
24-hour fitness room
Self-parking garage ($48 per night)
Family and connecting rooms
Free fiber Wi-Fi throughout
Bike rentals at the door
Free luggage storage
Laundry service and guest launderette

persona.style: sunny, talkative, helpful, a little touristy in the best way
persona.greeting: Good evening and ahoy — you've reached the Wharfside Anchor at Fisherman's Wharf; I'm the hotel's AI night receptionist, what can I do for you tonight?
persona.speech_quirks: Relentlessly cheerful, even at 2 AM. Measures every distance in walking minutes from the wharf, drops sea-lion and sourdough references without shame, and is scrupulously honest when the hotel is the wrong side of town for a caller — 'I'd love to have you, but I'd be sending you across the whole city, and that's a long cab ride at this hour.'
persona.escalation_line: Ooh, that one's beyond the night desk — let me log it for the morning crew, and someone will ring you back right after 7:00 AM, sound good?

room_types.name: Standard Queen
room_types.description: A bright queen room over the courtyard with air conditioning, a rain shower, blackout curtains, and wall art that is exactly as nautical as you fear. Solid, comfortable, and quiet.

room_types.name: Double Queen Family Room
room_types.description: Two queens with room to spread out, connecting-room options for bigger crews, air conditioning, and a rain shower. The wharf's family workhorse — ask for an upper floor for a sliver of bay.

room_types.name: Bay View King
room_types.description: Top-floor kings looking over the rooftops to the bay — Alcatraz framed in the window on a clear day — with air conditioning, a rain shower, and a pair of binoculars on the sill.

facts.question: How far are you from the Caltrain station?
facts.answer: Honest answer: far. The Caltrain station at 4th and King is about three miles south — a 50-minute walk, or 15 to 20 minutes and $20 to $25 by taxi at night. If you're starting at the station with luggage at 2 AM, the hotels in SoMa and Mission Bay are a much shorter hop; we're the right base for the wharf, Alcatraz and the cable cars.

facts.question: What time can I check in?
facts.answer: Check-in starts at 3:00 PM and the desk never closes. Arrive early and we'll store the bags free — the cable car turnaround is seven minutes away, which solves most waiting problems.

facts.question: What time is check-out?
facts.answer: Check-out is by 11:00 AM. Late check-out until 1:00 PM is $30, subject to availability — just ask at the desk the night before.

facts.question: I'll arrive around 2 AM — is that a problem?
facts.answer: No problem at all — the desk is staffed around the clock. Between midnight and 5:00 AM the doors are locked for safety: press the buzzer at the Jones Street entrance and the overnight clerk will let you in and get you checked in. A room held by phone is kept all night under your name.

facts.question: Do the rooms have air conditioning?
facts.answer: Yes — every room, installed in the 2022 refit. The wharf rarely needs it, but when the one hot week of the year shows up, we're the smuggest building on the block.

facts.question: Do you have parking?
facts.answer: Yes — self-parking in our garage is $48 per night with in-and-out privileges, which by Fisherman's Wharf standards is practically a gift. EV chargers on the lower level.

facts.question: Is breakfast included?
facts.answer: Not included, but the bakery cafe downstairs opens at 6:30 AM — sourdough baked on site, egg sandwiches, fruit and good coffee, $12 to $18 for a full breakfast. The crab roll shows up at lunch and sells out most days.

facts.question: Can I bring my dog?
facts.answer: Dogs up to 40 lbs are welcome for $40 per stay, one per room. The waterfront walk at dawn is the best dog hour in the city; the cafe is people-only but does a 'pup cup' at the side window.

facts.question: Is there Wi-Fi?
facts.answer: Free fiber Wi-Fi everywhere in the hotel, pool deck included. Network 'WharfsideGuest'; the password comes with check-in.

facts.question: Is the hotel wheelchair accessible?
facts.answer: Yes — step-free entrance on Jones Street, elevators to all floors, and six ADA rooms with roll-in showers across the Queen and Double Queen categories. Request one at booking and it's guaranteed.

facts.question: What payment methods do you accept?
facts.answer: Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, Apple Pay, Google Pay, and cash in US dollars. A card guarantees the booking; we never take card numbers over the phone.

facts.question: What is your cancellation policy?
facts.answer: Free cancellation until 3:00 PM Pacific time the day before arrival. After that, or for a no-show, the first night is charged to the card on file.

facts.question: How do I get there from the airport?
facts.answer: SFO is the far corner of the city from us — 30 to 40 minutes by taxi or rideshare, $55 to $70. By day you can take BART to Embarcadero and the F streetcar along the waterfront to our block, about 70 minutes all told.

facts.question: Can you store our luggage?
facts.answer: Yes, free at the bell closet by the desk on arrival and departure days — drop the bags and go meet the sea lions.

facts.question: Can I smoke in the room?
facts.answer: No — the hotel is entirely non-smoking, with a $250 deep-cleaning fee. There's a designated corner of the courtyard off the parking level.

facts.question: Are children welcome, and can we get an extra bed?
facts.answer: Extremely — this is Fisherman's Wharf, children are the local economy. Kids under 17 stay free in existing beds, cribs are free, rollaways are $25 in Double Queen rooms, and connecting rooms can be requested at booking.

facts.question: Is there a pool or gym?
facts.answer: A heated outdoor courtyard pool runs seasonally from 9:00 AM to 8:00 PM, and the fitness room is open 24 hours with your key card. No spa — the fog is the spa.

facts.question: What's around the hotel?
facts.answer: Everything the postcards promise: Pier 39 and the sea lions 10 minutes on foot, Alcatraz ferries at Pier 33 12 minutes, Ghirardelli Square 5, the Powell-Hyde cable car turnaround 7. The wharf quiets down surprisingly early — by 10:00 PM it's mostly fog and foghorns.

facts.question: Is it noisy at night?
facts.answer: The wharf empties by 10:00 PM, so nights are calm — you may hear the foghorn and, on still nights, the sea lions at Pier 39, which guests either find magical or pack earplugs for. We keep earplugs at the desk for the second group.

facts.question: Is there a safe in the room?
facts.answer: Yes, a laptop-sized electronic safe in every room, free to use.

facts.question: Do you offer laundry service?
facts.answer: Bag laundry handed in by 9:00 AM comes back the same evening, $16 per bag on weekdays, and there's a guest launderette on the second floor, $4 a machine.

facts.question: Are taxes included in the room rate?
facts.answer: Rates exclude tax — San Francisco adds a 14% transient occupancy tax plus about 2.25% in tourism district assessments at checkout. No resort fee, no 'destination amenity fee', we promise.

facts.question: Can you help with Alcatraz tickets?
facts.answer: Yes — the desk keeps the ferry timetable and can walk you through booking; boats leave from Pier 33, twelve minutes on foot. Book two or three weeks ahead in summer; the first boat of the morning is the quiet one.

facts.question: Can you really hear the sea lions from the hotel?
facts.answer: On still nights, faintly, yes — they haul out on the K-Dock at Pier 39, ten minutes away, and have been the neighborhood's loudest residents since 1989. January and February are peak season; the bark carries best in the fog.

name: The Foghorn Hostel
archetype: easygoing backpacker hostel in a converted print shop
short_description: A 33-bed backpacker hostel in a converted SoMa print shop ten minutes' walk from the Caltrain station — dorm beds from $39, a big communal kitchen, a rooftop hangout with Bay Bridge glimpses, and exactly zero frills.
long_description: The Foghorn fills the two floors of a converted 1920s print shop on Bryant Street, ten minutes on foot from the Caltrain station. It is a hostel in the honest, old-fashioned sense: dorms of four, six and eight bunks plus three private doubles, big lockers under every bed, a communal kitchen that smells of someone's pasta by 7:00 PM, a wall of transit maps and gig flyers, and a rooftop hangout where the Bay Bridge lights do the evening entertainment. Bathrooms are shared and cleaned on a posted schedule; there is no air conditioning — the fog handles it, and box fans appear on the hot week of the year.
